intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current physical state. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are indistinguishable or lost. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable specimen, though it has suffered significant decline.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil and a plant marker; no living tissue detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains eggshell debris; no identifiable mint or silver guest foliage.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot with white rabbit anchor; exhibits severe chlorosis and structural collapse of the primary leaf.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears consistently dry/compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the P2/P4 pot indicates an attempted nutrient intervention that has failed to support plant life.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial state shows a single Pothos leaf and a struggling stem in the P2/P4 pot.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Progressive wilting of the P2/P4 stem; Pothos leaf remains static but shows signs of dehydration.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Severe lighting shift to blue-spectrum/low-light; total loss of visual detail suggests plant senescence or complete collapse.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of photosynthetic activity and the presence of only a single, likely dying, Pothos leaf suggests the environment is unable to sustain the current specimens.
vision_report.anomalies: The introduction of eggshells as a calcium/nutrient source has not prevented the systemic loss of the specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a catastrophic decline. Starting from a state of minimal viability, the plants have progressively succumbed to what appears to be a combination of nutrient deficiency and environmental stress. The current state is characterized by near-total loss of biomass, with only a single, severely compromised Pothos leaf remaining as a vestige of the original registry.
